highways.name: Governor Ritchie Highway

honoree.name: Governor Ritchie
honoree.summary: Albert Ritchie was an American politician who served as the 25th Governor of Maryland from 1920 to 1935. Born in 1876, he was a prominent figure in the Democratic Party and ran for the presidential nomination in 1932. His political career also included service as Maryland's Attorney General. He is historically significant for his long tenure as governor and his opposition to Prohibition.
honoree.education: Johns Hopkins University, University of Maryland School of Law

highways.name: Aris T. Allen Boulevard

honoree.name: Aris T. Allen
honoree.summary: Aris T. Allen was an American physician and Republican politician from Maryland. He was born on August 21, 1921, in Annapolis, Maryland, and died on June 21, 1991, in the same city. He served in the Maryland House of Delegates and was the first African American to be nominated for governor by a major political party in Maryland. He was also a prominent civic leader and a member of the Anne Arundel County Board of Education.
honoree.education: Hampton Institute, University of Maryland School of Medicine

highways.name: Francis Scott Key Highway

honoree.name: Francis Scott Key
honoree.summary: Francis Scott Key was born on August 1, 1779, in Carroll County, Maryland, and died on January 11, 1843, in Baltimore, Maryland. He was an American lawyer and author who wrote the poem that became the lyrics for the United States national anthem, 'The Star-Spangled Banner.' Key witnessed the Battle of Fort McHenry during the War of 1812, which inspired his famous writing. He also served as a U.S. Attorney and was involved in various legal and religious activities in his lifetime.

Augustine Herman Highway

honoree.name: Augustine Herman
honoree.summary: Augustine Herman was a 17th-century Bohemian-born merchant, cartographer, and diplomat who became a prominent landowner in the English colony of Maryland. He is best known for creating the detailed 'Nova Belgica et Anglia Nova' map of the mid-Atlantic region in 1670. Herman secured a large land grant from the Maryland proprietor, establishing the Bohemia Manor plantation. His work was significant for early American colonial geography and diplomacy.

John Hanson Highway

honoree.name: John Hanson
honoree.summary: John Hanson was born in 1721 in Charles County, Maryland, and died in 1783 in Upper Marlboro, Maryland. He was a merchant and politician who served as the first President of the Continental Congress under the Articles of Confederation. His political career included roles in the Maryland colonial legislature and the Continental Congress. He is historically significant as an early statesman in the American Revolutionary period.

George Washington Highway

honoree.name: George Washington
honoree.summary: George Washington was born on February 22, 1732, in Westmoreland County, Virginia, and died on December 14, 1799, in Mount Vernon, Virginia. He served as the first President of the United States from 1789 to 1797 and previously commanded the Continental Army during the American Revolutionary War. His leadership was instrumental in securing American independence and establishing key precedents for the new republic. He is widely regarded as one of the most influential Founding Fathers in United States history.

Dedicated to Governor Robert L. Ehrlich, Jr.

honoree.name: Robert L. Ehrlich
honoree.summary: Bob Ehrlich is an American politician and attorney who served as the 60th Governor of Maryland from 2003 to 2007. Born on November 25, 1957, in Baltimore, Maryland, he previously represented Maryland's 2nd congressional district in the U.S. House of Representatives. He is a member of the Republican Party and ran unsuccessfully for governor again in 2010. Ehrlich is notable for being Maryland's first Republican governor in over 30 years.
honoree.education: Duke University, BA, University of Maryland School of Law, JD
